title: CBP Server and Client Configuration
description: Configuration schema for the CBP server and client layers. Frame-level configuration is defined separately in frame.schema.json.

serverConfig.description: Server-level configuration governing all frames hosted by this CBP instance.

max_nodes_per_frame.description: Maximum number of nodes a single frame may contain before the server rejects further inserts.

max_depth.description: Maximum inheritance depth (lineage chain length from leaf to frame root).

max_conversations.description: Maximum number of distinct conversations the Frame Router retains tier-negotiation state for. The conversation id is client-supplied (X-CBP-Conversation), so this bounds memory against a client cycling unique ids; the least-recently-used conversation is evicted when the cap is exceeded.

default_decay.description: Decay policy applied to nodes that do not declare one and whose lineage chain provides no inherited value.

epoch_interval_seconds.description: Wall-clock interval in seconds at which the GC sweep runs. Each sweep advances the server's monotonic epoch counter by exactly one. The epoch counter — not wall clock — is the semantic time source for decay:epoch nodes.

decay_factor.description: Multiplier applied to a node's w on each epoch advance when the node has decay:epoch. At default 0.85, a node with w=1.0 falls below the GC threshold of 0.1 after approximately 14 epochs.

strategy.description: GC strategy. 'prune_below_weight' removes nodes whose w falls below the threshold. Future strategies will be added via spec amendment.

threshold.description: Weight below which a node is pruned on the next GC sweep.

gc_policy.description: Garbage collection policy. Determines when decayed nodes are removed from the graph.

edge_vocabulary.description: Edge vocabulary level. 'standard_8' is the 8 edges defined in Section III. 'extended_12' adds 4 future edges (not yet specified — reserved for a future spec amendment).

condensed_threshold.description: Minimum ratio of changed fields to total fields before a Condensed tier serialization is triggered (vs resending Full).

signal_min_turns.description: Minimum number of conversation turns that must have received a Full serialization before Signal tier is permitted.

compression.description: Tier compression thresholds governing when the serializer transitions between Full, Condensed, and Signal tiers.

driver.description: Storage driver. 'memory' keeps the graph in-process only (restart = clean slate). 'filesystem' snapshots the graph to disk with debounced atomic writes.

path.description: Absolute path to the snapshot file when driver='filesystem'. Required in that case. The server hydrates from this file on start and writes atomically to this path (via a .tmp sibling + rename).

persistence.description: Durability configuration (v0.5). When driver='filesystem', the server serializes the graph to the configured path ~1s after the last mutation using canonicalize() output.

clientConfig.description: Client-level configuration for a CBP consumer (LLM app, agent, dashboard).

preferred_tier.description: 'auto' lets the server decide tier based on conversation state. Specific values force a tier.

context_window_budget.description: Total tokens the client reserves for CBP across all frames in a single request. The Frame Router distributes budget across active frames.

multi_frame_strategy.description: 'merge' = combine multiple frames into one payload. 'priority_stack' = deliver frames in priority order until budget exhausted. 'isolate' = each frame gets its own payload block.

stale_frame_policy.description: What happens when a cached frame's TTL has expired. 'warn' = deliver with staleness marker. 'refresh' = fetch fresh before delivery. 'use_cached' = deliver stale silently.

write_access.description: Whether this client can upsert nodes into the graph. False = read-only consumer. True = agents that record their own outputs as prior nodes (Mode 4: embedded SDK).
